title: atriplex crispa
intro: Atriplex crispa, also known as bladder saltbush, is a type of shrub that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family and is native to Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Atriplex crispa include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria. Herbal preparations of Atriplex crispa can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
